title: 429 responses trigger an unbounded tight retry loop in webhook delivery
product_summary: Acme webhook delivery service (Node/Express): subscribers register HTTPS endpoints and receive signed platform events

ground_truth.root_cause: dispatchWebhook() in src/dispatcher.js treats a 429 response as a free retry: it neither increments the attempt counter nor sleeps before redelivering, and the loop condition (attempt <= maxRetries || status === 429) keeps looping while the target throttles. Any endpoint that rate-limits gets hammered in a tight loop with unbounded redeliveries — customers see thousands of requests per minute and duplicate deliveries; maxRetries (including 0) is never honored under throttling.
ground_truth.fix_contract: Every delivery attempt counts against the maxRetries bound regardless of status code (429 included): one initial attempt plus at most maxRetries retries, and maxRetries=0 means exactly one attempt. Retries wait with exponential backoff (with jitter) between attempts instead of redelivering immediately. A target that returns 429 twice and then 200 receives exactly 3 deliveries with a backoff pause before each retry, so duplicate floods cease.
expected_evidence: burst of webhook_delivery_attempt events with status=429 (thousands within minutes) while webhook_delivered stays flat
attempt-to-delivered ratio exploding for the affected endpoints, normal for endpoints that never return 429
distractors: the customer's endpoint or WAF being misconfigured (their rate limiting is the trigger, not the fault)
HMAC signature verification in src/signatures.js causing rejected deliveries
